questions.question: Which scheme of providing additional food-grain to NFSA beneficiaries, has been extended till November?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet has recently approved further allocation of additional foodgrain to National Food Security Act (NFSA) beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Yojana (Phase IV) till November. Under the scheme, the government will provide 5 kg foodgrains per person per month free of cost to maximum 81.35 crore beneficiaries covered under National Food Security Act (NFSA), including those covered under Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

questions.question: Which country supplied Dragunov sniper rifles to the Indian Army?
questions.explanation: Indian Army procured Dragunov sniper rifles from Russia in the 1990s. The rifles have fallen behind contemporary snipers used across the world. Recently, Russia agreed to offer the Indian Army, a comprehensive upgrade of the Dragunov sniper rifle. They are in service with almost all frontline battalions of the Army. A demonstration is also being planned to showcase improved accuracy of the modified guns.

questions.question: Who is the composer of the official theme song of the Indian Olympic Team of Tokyo 2020?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports Kiren Rijiju launched the official theme song of the Indian Olympic Team of Tokyo 2020. The song ‘Tu Thaan Le’, has been composed and sung by popular playback singer Mohit Chauhan. Over 100 athletes are expected to represent India at the Tokyo Games.

questions.question: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is the chief of which organisation?
questions.explanation: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is the Chief of World Health Organisation (WHO). Recently, the leaders of the World Health Organization (WHO), the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) and the World Trade Organization (WTO) announced a joint platform to help countries access Covid-19 vaccines, treatments and technologies.

questions.question: Which species produces the highly demanded substance named ‘Ambergris’, from the digestive system?
questions.explanation: Ambergris, or grey amber, is a solid waxy substance of grey colour produced in the digestive system of sperm whales. Also called as whale vomit or floating gold, it is highly demanded in the international market. Over 9 kg of Ambergris was seized by the Mumbai Police over the past 15 days. It is used to create fragrances like musk and in some traditional medicines.

questions.question: Giant muntjac, a critically endangered animal, has been spotted in Virachey National Park, of which country?
questions.explanation: Giant muntjac, a rare barking deer has been spotted in Cambodia for the first time, in its Virachey National Park. Muntiacus vuquangensis is on the red list of the International Union for Conservation of Nature as critically endangered. It is known for its long antlers. The species was first found in forests in neighbouring countries of Vietnam and Laos in 1994.

questions.question: Which Indian state has declared itself as the first Rabies-free state?
questions.explanation: Goa Chief Minister Pramod Sawant announced that the state has not reported a single rabies case in the last three years. This makes Goa the first rabies-free state in the country. The state has achieved over 5 lakh vaccinations against the rabies in dogs. It also has set up 24-hours rabies surveillance with an emergency hotline and rapid response team for dog bite victims.

questions.question: Which Indian state has been adjudged as the top performer in the Smart City awards 2020?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has declared the results of the India Smart Cities Awards Contest (ISAC) 2020, under which Uttar Pradesh stood as the top performer followed by Madhya Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. Among the cities, Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and Surat (Gujarat) have been awarded jointly for their overall development.

questions.question: Which pharma company manufactures the DRDO drug 2-DG to treat Covid patients?
questions.explanation: 2-deoxy-D-glucose (2-DG) was developed by the Institute of Nuclear Medicine & Allied Sciences (INMAS), a laboratory of the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), in collaboration with Dr Reddy’s company. Dr Reddy’s Laboratories (DRL) has commercially launched 2-DG drug, an oral drug used to treat moderate to severe Covid patients.

questions.question: Rosatom, which is in the news recently, is a state-owned nuclear energy company of which country?
questions.explanation: Rosatom is the Russian state-owned nuclear power corporation. This company is in news recently because the corporation has commenced the construction of a fifth reactor building at India’s Kudankulam nuclear power plant, in Tamil Nadu. The proposed nuclear reactor is said to be one of the most advanced reactors in the world and would be built with state-of-the-art technologies.
